channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: King Charles III: ‘A Partnership Born Out of Dispute’
item.description: The British monarch praises multilateral institutions in a pointed message to the Trump administration.

item.title: King Charles III Delivers Pointed Message in Address to U.S. Congress
item.description: The British monarch praised multilateral institutions, warned against climate change, and championed diversity.

item.title: China Pulls the Plug on Meta’s AI Acquisition
item.description: The reversal underscores Beijing’s shifting national security concerns.

item.title: Why DeepSeek’s new model has been met with a shrug
item.description: The AI lab faces stiffening competition and a meddling state

item.title: Europe needs Ukraine to fight Russia
item.description: It is risky to let Ukraine into the EU. It’s more dangerous to keep it out

item.title: The UAE’s departure from OPEC may not break the cartel
item.description: But it highlights longstanding tensions that the Gulf war has exacerbated

item.title: Coca-Cola is trouncing Pepsi. Can the underdog turn things around?
item.description: The maker of the world’s second-favourite fizzy drink is becoming more like its rival

item.title: Why Iran Isn’t Blinking Yet
item.description: Trump’s blockade aims to force damaging shutdowns at Iranian oil fields. But Tehran has been through this before.

item.title: A global fight over banking rules is just getting started
item.description: Co-operation on the financial system will be the casualty

item.title: Japan and China Are Edging Dangerously Close to Conflict
item.description: Beijing is ready to take risks as Tokyo backs Taiwan.

item.title: How to protect France from an Orban-style takeover
item.description: What if the National Rally wins, in one of Europe’s most centralised states?

item.title: The EU Needs New Security Partners
item.description: Europe’s security order needs to be reconstituted. In a world of fracturing alliances, closer security partnerships with a wider range of states, however small, will increasingly become a source of power, allowing Europe to defend its interests wherever they are at stake.

item.title: Asia’s Economic Diplomacy for Tumultuous Times
item.description: Instead of allowing external powers to dictate their development, China, India, Japan, South Korea, and Vietnam have balanced strategic international engagement with domestic capacity-building and decision-making. This model has become newly relevant for Canada, the United Kingdom, and other middle powers.

item.title: Expanding Frontiers: China’s Military Push Beyond the First Island Chain
item.description: Beijing has a deliberate, long-term strategy – one that prioritizes persistence and gradual advantage.

item.title: Whether Kusong or Yongbyon, North Korea Will Never Give Up Its Nuclear Weapons Program
item.description: A controversy over the South Korean unification minister’s comments exposes the illusion at the heart of decades of North Korea policy.

item.title: What’s Wrong With the SAVE America Act?
item.description: Much of the discussion about US President Donald Trump’s proposed SAVE America Act has centered on the bill’s partisan electoral implications. But given the inevitability of false positives, Americans should be asking a more fundamental question: Am I willing to give up my own right to vote to keep a non-citizen from voting?

item.title: Is the Indian Army Ready for the Next War?
item.description: Col. Ajai Shukla discusses India’s Army modernization and reform post-Sindoor.

item.title: High Oil Prices Are Devastating Island States – and Moving the Needle on Climate
item.description: For many small island states, the abstract concept of a “polycrisis” has become a dangerously tangible threat.

item.title: The Perils of South Korea’s Digital Diplomacy
item.description: Lee Jae-myung’s post on X marked a new beginning for South Korea’s digital diplomacy. It immediately caused a diplomatic row.

item.title: What Does Australia’s National Defense Strategy Mean for the Quad?
item.description: The implications of Australia’s NDS for the Indo-Pacific region and the Quad warrant careful examination.

item.title: South Korea’s Precarious Balancing Act
item.description: Caught between US President Donald Trump’s unpredictable administration and a rising China, South Korea is highly exposed to geopolitical turmoil and energy shocks. With American reliability in doubt, it must now safeguard its export-driven economy and build its own capabilities while navigating an increasingly unstable world.

item.title: China’s Belt and Road Initiative in Bangladesh, 10 Years Later
item.description: It’s been almost a decade since Bangladesh joined the BRI. What has the Belt and Road actually accomplished in the country?

item.title: Could Russia Mediate the Conflict Between Pakistan and Afghanistan?
item.description: Moscow is the latest to offer, however cautiously and conditionally, to mediate between Pakistan and Afghanistan.

item.title: Russia’s War Boom Masks an Economic Implosion
item.description: Record-low unemployment is the result of millions of missing workers.

item.title: Russia’s War Boom Masks an Economic Implosion
item.description: Record-low unemployment is the result of millions of missing workers.

item.title: Sun Tzu’s Advice for Donald Trump
item.description: Amid a series of massive, illegal policy blunders, US President Donald Trump has set his sights on stabilizing relations with Chinese President Xi Jinping at their May summit. But it will probably go poorly because Trump is incapable of understanding the importance of strategy, a lesson that China's leaders have long grasped.

item.title: Central Asia’s Next Growth Story Runs on Water It Does Not Have
item.description: Kazakhstan and Uzbekistan are committing billions to mining, nuclear power, data centers and AI infrastructure at the same time that the region’s water supply is becoming less abundant, less predictable and more legally constrained.

item.title: Iran Is More Unified Than Ever
item.description: The war has deepened Iranian officials’ connections with one another—and with the public.

item.title: How Gwadar Port Is Benefiting From the Strait of Hormuz Crisis
item.description: Business at the port has increased amid ongoing tension. But business should not have to depend on disruptions and wars.

item.title: Indian Political Parties Go All Out to Court Women in Assembly Elections
item.description: With women comprising around half the electorate, politicians have promised them direct cash transfers and gas cylinders. Will it impact how women vote?

item.title: ASEAN’s Trade With Europe: The Costs and Trials of Doing Business
item.description: A conversation with Chris Humphrey, executive director of the EU-ASEAN Business Council.

item.title: Latin America’s Anti-Women Movement Is Spreading
item.description: Chile's president José Antonio Kast is following the regressive examples set elsewhere in the region.

item.title: The Indian Ocean Has Too Many Forums and Too Few Deliverables
item.description: Regional forums often celebrate participation, but participation alone is not a policy outcome.

item.title: What Congress Could Do to Stop the War
item.description: Republicans are declining to use their power of the purse.

item.title: The Iran War Is Tearing Trump’s Coalition Apart
item.description: MAGA is not necessarily the same thing as America First.

item.title: The Disposable Oligarchs
item.description: Why wealthy elites come to regret their bargains with authoritarians.

item.title: Let Iran Defeat Itself
item.description: America should end the war but keep up the pressure.

item.title: Myanmar Is Not ‘in Transition’: War by Other Means and the Risks of Policy Drift
item.description: Rather than focusing on elite-level political maneuvering, foreign governments need to recognize the transformative potential of the country's resistance movement.

item.title: Indian Air Force’s Push to Manufacture Aero Engines and Fighter Aircraft
item.description: The indigenously manufactured Tejas Mark 1A would be a viable competitor in the international market for light fighters.

item.title: Former Malaysian PM Najib Withdraws Appeal Against House Arrest Ruling: Report
item.description: The decision is a setback for the ex-leader, who was recently sentenced to an additional 15 years in prison for his central involvement in the 1MDB corruption scandal.

item.title: EU Extends Sanctions Against Myanmar, Citing ‘Continuing Grave Situation’
item.description: The announcement comes as the country's new quasi-civilian government pursues diplomatic normalization with the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN).

item.title: Iran Crisis: Pakistan’s Prominence and India’s Strategic Silence
item.description: While Pakistan has stepped up to try and mediate between the U.S. and Iran, India has taken a much quieter approach.

item.title: Watching Iran, China Hopes to Learn New Tricks for the Taiwan Strait
item.description: Beijing is learning a lot from Iran’s Hormuz Strait closure and how it could apply Tehran’s methods to a future conflict with the U.S. over Taiwan.
